name: Poblano and Smoked Chicken Chowder with Hominy
recipeIngredient: 1 Pound yellow onions, halved and sliced lengthwise
3 Medium poblano chiles, seeded and sliced into thin strips
1/2 cup tomatoes (drained if using canned), seeded and diced
2 cups fruity white wine,  see note
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add the onions, poblanos, and garlic to the saucepan. Sauté until soft but not brown, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the tomatillos, fennel seed, cumin seed, oregano, cinnamon, tomatoes, stock, and wine. Stir gently.
4. Bring the mixture to a simmer over low heat and let it cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Add the smoked chicken and hominy to the pot. Simmer until heated through, about 10-15 minutes more.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Garnish with chopped cilantro, diced avocado, and fresh lime juice just before serving.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
